ksa_text: Skill in evaluating emerging data and AI technologies for strategic relevance, assessing maturity, organizational fit, competitive impact, and integration requirements before recommending investment.

dcwf.top_element_label: Skill in conducting audits or reviews of technical systems.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA LS-S-011 (Leadership & Strategy/Skill) scores 0.504 composite (highest: NIST AI RMF at 0.504). Scope is edge_case for Chief Medical Information Officer: 0 in-scope signals, 0 out-of-scope signals. Seniority is appropriate.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Include consistent with edge_case scope, Leadership & Strategy, composite 0.504. Decision stands.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence support Leadership & Strategy for Chief Medical Information Officer?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Leadership & Strategy appears in approximately 78% of Chief Medical Information Officer job postings. Health system leadership and strategy core to CMIO executive mandate.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Include upheld. Leadership & Strategy is market-validated for Chief Medical Information Officer. Final decision: include.
